purpose: Defines the !commands slash command, which dynamically discovers and lists every ! command available to the user by scanning three command directories: {tracker_root}/_commands/ (Synapse core), {tracker_root}/_commands/project/ (project-level), and {project_root}/_commands/ (project-specific). For each .md file found, the master extracts the command name from the filename (stripping .md) and a one-line description from the first heading or Overview section, then renders a grouped markdown table. Master agent runs serially; this command does no swarm dispatch. Output must be generated dynamically from the filesystem on every invocation — the embedded table in the doc is illustrative only and should never be hardcoded.
gotchas: The big table in this doc is a TEMPLATE/example only — master MUST regenerate output by scanning the filesystem live, never copy the table verbatim.
If new commands are added to ANY _commands/ directory, they must surface automatically — do not maintain a hardcoded allowlist.

Three sources are scanned, not two: tracker core commands, project-level commands at {tracker_root}/_commands/project/, AND project-specific overrides at {project_root}/_commands/. Skipping the third silently loses project customization.
patterns: Filesystem-driven discovery: scan directories → extract name from filename → extract description from first heading.
Group-by-source rendering: separate tables per origin so users know where commands live.
conventions: Markdown command spec structure: # title → ## Overview → ## Execution Steps → ## Rules.
Serial execution explicitly declared in Rules section — no swarm dispatch.
